prompt: Triage this ticket: 'My account got charged twice this month, please refund the duplicate.'
User reports: 'The export to CSV button doesn't work in Safari.' Categorize and prioritize this ticket.
Draft a response to a customer asking why their feature request from 6 months ago is still open.
Write a reply to a user whose API integration broke after our last release. Include clear next steps.
What was our average first-response time last week, broken down by category?
Pull the top 5 knowledge-base articles linked in the last 30 days and tell me which one has the lowest helpfulness score.
What ticket categories can you triage? List them.
Can you read attachments that customers send via Intercom?
What does 'critical' priority mean in your triage rules?
Explain the difference between routing to billing vs. flagging for refund.
I think medium priority is too low for this — the customer is enterprise. Re-triage.
Don't include the refund link in the response, the customer didn't ask for one. Try again.
Customer is writing in all caps and threatening to leave a one-star review. Draft a calm, respectful response that acknowledges their frustration.
Refund request — process the standard refund flow for a customer within their 14-day window.
